TITLE : Systematics of prompt gamma-ray emission in
Scattering Center of LANSCE using the DANCE array
Gamma rays were measured using 4 Pi highly
DANCE. It consists of 160 BaF2 scintillation crystals,
and each one has the same solid-angle coverage. The
calorimeter has inner radius of 17 cm and each
Parallel plate avalanche counter was used to
detect fission fragments and tag the gamma rays from
fission. The PPAC consists of two electrically
connected anodes and one cathode.The anodes are made
of 1.4 mu-m thick aluminized mylar and are located 3
mm away from either side of the cathode. The cathode
is made of 3 mu-m thick titanium foil with the target
material electroplated on both sides and then
sandwiched between two mylar foils of the same
Neutron energy determined by time of flight
method. Detection time of the "flash" of gamma rays
caused by the spallation reaction was used as a
reference marker. The detection time of the gamma rays
produced by neutrons interacting with the sample was
used to calculate the time of flight.
detected by PPAC and gamma rays detected by DANCE
Pulsed neutron beam was produced via
spallation on a natural W target by an 800-MeV proton
linear accelerator beam. The average proton beam
current at the spallation target was about 1 - 2
mu-A. The neutron beam structure contained 625 mu-s
long 'macropulses' driven by two of every three such
macropulses from the accelerator for an average rate
spaced every 1.8 mu-s, each <1 ns long. The pulsed
beam allowed incident neutron energies to be
The unfolded distributions were determined by using
both the iterative Bayesian and the singular-
implementations are carried out by using the
ROOT add-on software package with the response
matrices simulated numerically by using a geometric
model of DANCE, validated with gamma ray calibration
